summary: Excellent Betty Cantor soundboard captures a stellar May ’77 show highlighted by an iconic Peggy‑O and soaring Scarlet > Fire.
review: The 5/5/77 New Haven recording is a superb example of the band’s peak year – crystal‑clear Betty‑board fidelity lets every nuance shine, from Jerry’s searing Peggy‑O solo to the blazing Scarlet > Fire jam.  The setlist flows effortlessly, with first‑set gems like Sugaree and Music Never Stopped leading into a second‑set packed with Estimated, St. Stephen and other fan‑favorite improvisations.  While a few reviewers note that Fire and Lazy Lightning are weaker points, the overwhelming consensus is that this performance ranks among the best of the ’77 run.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 reviews, the vast majority are 5‑star with consistent praise for both performance and sound quality; only a handful of mild criticisms exist.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty Cantor’s board work is praised as crystal‑clear, well‑balanced, with superb separation and minimal tape noise; occasional minor warble/dropout noted on other versions but not on the primary SBD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight first set that builds momentum with Sugaree and MNS, followed by a second‑set jam marathon (Estimated → Scarlet>Fire → St. Stephen) that epitomizes the fluid, experimental vibe of spring 1977.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for his soaring solos on Peggy-O, Sugaree and St. Stephen; described as “on fire” and emotionally powerful.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for thumping bass lines and tight rhythmic foundation throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: His vocal delivery and guitar interplay with Jerry are highlighted as a highlight of the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano/organ work is noted positively (especially on Estimated and Scarlet>Fire) though a few reviewers wish for more prominent keys.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey receive brief nods for tight, thundering drumming that drives the jams.
